description: Marks motion that crosses a boundary to enter an interior space. Where 'mua' (in) indicates static location within, 'muo' captures the dynamic crossing—the threshold moment of entry, the transition from outside to inside. This preposition honors the significance of entering: homes, communities, states of being, new understanding.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'mu', sharing the interior-marking sounds of its sibling 'mua' (in)—the nasal 'm' grounding and the rounded 'u' suggesting enclosed space. But where 'mua' opens to spacious 'a' for static containment, 'muo' closes toward 'o', the vowel of completion and arrival. Speaking 'muo' creates the acoustic shape of entry: from grounded beginning through rounded interior to the 'o' of having arrived inside. The minimal pair with 'mue' (out of) through vowel alternation makes directional meaning audible.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a directional preposition indicating motion crossing a boundary inward. Precedes its object: 'muo nuroa' (into the kitchen), 'muo phialu' (into the water). Distinguished from 'mua' (in) which marks static location. Often implies transformation or state change when used metaphorically: 'muo nophi' (into wisdom). Can combine with verbs of motion or be implied by context.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Entry marks transformation—entering meditation, entering understanding, entering community. 'Muo' grammaticalizes this recognition that crossing thresholds matters, that the moment of entering deserves linguistic attention.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Entering another's space—physical or metaphorical—requires awareness. 'Muo' marks this crossing explicitly, supporting mindful transitions rather than unreflective intrusion.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional cultures marked entries with rituals—entering homes, sacred spaces, new life stages. 'Muo' carries this understanding that boundaries exist to be crossed consciously, not ignored.
